channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: CDC eyes narrower COVID-19 vaccine guidance ahead of 2025–2026 season
item.description: The CDC's vaccine committee will convene for a two-day meeting this week to consider recommendations on vaccines, including the COVID-19 vaccine for 2025-2026.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: West Point graduate becomes the first woman to compete in the grueling Army Ranger contest
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A female Army Ranger for the first time competed in the annual Best Ranger Competition, and her two-soldier team finished the grueling three-day event over the weekend and came in 14th overall. First Lt. Gabrielle White, and her teammate, Capt.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Medicaid puzzle confronts Minnesota lawmakers. Federal cuts could hit health safety net program
item.description: Republicans in Washington are working on slashing the federal budget to help pay for a slate of tax breaks. State lawmakers know it could mean a health care funding hole, but they don’t know how much they’ll have to backfill.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: A whistleblower’s disclosure details how DOGE may have taken sensitive labor data
item.description: A whistleblower tells Congress and NPR that DOGE may have taken sensitive labor data and hid its tracks. “None of that information should ever leave the agency,” said a former NLRB official.
item.dc:creator: Jenna McLaughlin, MPR News

item.title: Shapiro arson attack underscores threats to politicians
item.description: As more details emerge, experts said they’ll be watching for responses from top political figures on both sides of the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Top Republican warns families of 'largest tax hike in history' next year if Trump budget fails
item.description: Republican Study Committee Chairman August Pfluger is warning of a tax increase if the GOP fails to extend Trump's tax cuts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In Pelican Rapids, teens learn the skill of civil discourse
item.description: In a deeply polarized country, a new program in Minnesota is teaching middle and high schoolers how to find common ground in their communities. Program leaders say it’s an effort to inspire young people to become engaged politically and in their towns.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, after the institution said it would defy the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to address disability advocates in first major speech since leaving White House
item.description: Former President Biden is expected Tuesday to deliver his first public speech since leaving the White House, addressing Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Republican campaign arm touts 'unstoppable momentum' with record fundraising haul
item.description: As it aims to defend its razor-thin majority in the House of Representatives in next year's midterm elections, the National Republican Congressional Committee is off to a fast fundraising start.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Philadelphia prosecutor seeks a third term, saying progressivism is ascendant despite Trump setbacks
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — The Philadelphia prosecutor who became a campaign trail punching bag for Republicans amid rising crime in U.S. cities during the coronavirus pandemic is running for reelection on the back end of a withering effort to oust progressive district attorneys nationwide.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP senator reveals why Trump's 'complete and total endorsement' will be crucial for his midterm race
item.description: Fox News Digital spoke to Ohio GOP Sen. Jon Husted about receiving the endorsement of President Donald Trump for next year's election and what he hopes to accomplish going forward.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big law spends top dollar as Trump takes aim at firms
item.description: A series of new agreements brokered between President Trump and major law firms suggests a new price tag for those looking to dodge executive orders targeting the legal profession.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tehran says next round of Iran-US nuclear talks in Oman after Rome named by officials
item.description: ROME (AP) — Talks between Iran and the United States over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program appeared ready to leave the Middle East on Monday, as an Italian source and others said the next round of negotiations would take place in Rome.
item.dc:creator: MARIA GRAZIA MURRU, JON GAMBRELL and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Joe Biden will speak about Social Security in his return to the national stage
item.description: Former President Joe Biden is returning to the national stage to help elevate concerns that President Donald Trump’s agenda is threatening the health of Social Security.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples And Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Despite a court order, White House bars AP from Oval Office event
item.description: Despite a court order, a reporter and photographer from The Associated Press were barred from an Oval Office news conference on Monday with President Donald Trump and his counterpart from El Salvador, Nayib Bukele.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House proposes drastic cuts to State Department and funding for UN, NATO and other groups
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House’s Office of Management and Budget has proposed gutting the State Department’s budget by almost 50%, closing a number of overseas diplomatic missions, slashing the number of diplomatic staff, and eliminating funding for nearly all international organizations, includi
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he wants to imprison US citizens in El Salvador. That's likely illegal
item.description: President Donald Trump keeps talking about sending U.S. citizens who commit violent crimes to prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pritzker says he's undecided about testifying before Congressional panel
item.description: The US House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform requested testimony from Pritzker, other Democrats about sanctuary laws.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: El Salvador President Bukele says he won't be releasing a Maryland man back to the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's top advisers and Nayib Bukele, the president of El Salvador, said Monday that they have no basis for the small Central American nation to return a Maryland man who was wrongly deported there last month.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM and MARCOS ALEMÁN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former President Joe Biden to speak in Chicago on Tuesday in effort to rally support for Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden is scheduled to speak at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: US Army to control land on Mexico border as part of base, migrants could be detained, officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A long sliver of federal land along the U.S.-Mexico border that President Donald Trump is turning over to the Department of Defense would be controlled by the Army as part of a base, which could allow troops to detain any trespassers, including migrants, U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Getting the IRS on the phone is more difficult this tax filing season, experts say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Taxpayers calling the IRS for help processing their taxes this filing season may find it harder than normal to get someone on the phone, experts say, a problem that is only expected to get worse next year with staffing cuts that could slash the workforce considerably.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump sued over 'Liberation Day' tariffs
item.description: President Trump’s “Liberation Day” tariffs came under their first major legal challenge Monday, brought by a libertarian public-interest firm that argues the president overstepped his authority.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Minneapolis council member Emily Koski drops out of mayor's race
item.description: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski has dropped out of the mayor’s race, according to a campaign update Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Trump pharma tariff threat raises specter of shortages, price hikes
item.description: The pharmaceutical industry is bracing for chaos if President Trump follows through on his threat to impose "a major tariff" on prescription drug imports.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Sanders jokingly calls Ocasio-Cortez his daughter at Utah rally
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) joked during a joint appearance at a Utah rally on Sunday that Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) was his daughter, praising the progressive lawmaker and highlighting her background.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: What to know as Social Security anti-fraud measures take effect
item.description: Individuals who are flagged will be required to prove their identity in person for their Social Security claim to be processed.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Bill Maher raves about dinner with Trump, Geraldo Rivera weighs in
item.description: Rivera was criticized when he appeared on “Real Time with Bill Maher” and said the president can be friendly.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's commerce secretary says new electronics tariff exemptions are temporary, chip tariffs coming
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tariff exemptions announced Friday on electronics like smartphones and laptops are only a temporary reprieve until the Trump administration develops a new tariff approach specific to the semiconductor industry, U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Military schools offer test case for Trump education reforms
item.description: Military academies could increasingly show what President Trump wants to see from public schools and colleges.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tariffs bring Democrat disagreements to surface
item.description: President Donald Trump’s tariffs are putting Democrats in a challenging political position as they decide how strongly to denounce his trade policy.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, except China
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
